description:
This map displays several Natura 2000 (N2K) sites and their corresponding buffered Areas of Interest (AoI), likely located in France given the "FRZ" prefix in the site codes.
The colour coding indicates:
*   Light blue shaded polygons: Natura 2000 sites, identified by unique codes such as FRZ200683, FRZ200684, FRZ200685, FRZ200686, and FRZ200682 (partially visible).
*   Light orange shaded polygons: The Natura 2000 Area of Interest (AoI), which encompasses the N2K site and an associated 2 km buffer zone.
*   Dark grey lines within site FRZ200685: Represent hydrological features, likely rivers or streams, integrated into the N2K site definition.

The spatial pattern illustrates how a single Natura 2000 site code can refer to multiple geographically detached areas, known as "multi-part polygons." For instance, site FRZ200683 comprises three distinct light blue polygons, each surrounded by its own light orange AoI buffer. Similarly, FRZ200684 is composed of two separate light blue polygons, each buffered. FRZ200685 represents a linear, dendritic N2K site, characteristic of a river system, with its encompassing AoI. FRZ200686 shows a larger, irregularly shaped N2K site and its buffer. No scale bar, compass, legend, or data source information is explicitly visible on the map.
